commands.author: zhangguiping-xydt

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): align agent_end hook timeout with max reviewer timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: after `origin/main` removed the old `extensions/skill-workshop` runtime, Skill Research auto-capture still ran as an awaited core `agent_end` side effect for local CLI runs through `awaitAgentEndSideEffects`, so capture work could still delay routine session close even though plugin hook timeouts were managed separately.
- Solution: start Skill Research auto-capture as a non-blocking side effect on both fire-and-forget and awaited agent-end paths, while preserving error logging and still awaiting the plugin hook path when callers require it.
- Scope: the rebased fix now targets the current `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` runtime path instead of resurrecting the deleted `extensions/skill-workshop` package.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Skill Research auto-capture no longer blocks awaited `agent_end` completion; awaited local CLI runs still wait for real plugin `agent_end` hooks, and channel-backed CLI runs still do not.
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Routine local CLI/session-close paths should not wait on delayed Skill Research auto-capture work before returning final agent output.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktree at `ea94671edac14d37dd2524c3a92fc50810827e3b`; isolated dev gateway startup on port 19175; isolated HOME/state/workspace real Gateway run using `openclaw gateway run` and `openclaw gateway call agent --expect-final` with a local mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server as provider `openai`, model `gpt-5.5`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts -t "waits for agent_end hooks before resolving successful CLI runs|does not wait for agent_end hooks before resolving channel-backed CLI runs"`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `OPENCLAW_REPO="$TASK_WORKTREE" OPENCLAW_LOG="$EVIDENCE_DIR/openclaw-dev-gateway.log" OPENCLAW_DEV_PID_FILE="$EVIDENCE_DIR/openclaw-dev.pid" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19175 OPENCLAW_DEV_AUTO_PAIR=false /home/0668001030/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh`
  - `node "$EVIDENCE_DIR/real-gateway-delayed-autocapture-proof.mjs"`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - `vitest-agent-end-side-effects.log`: 1 file passed, 3 tests passed.
  - `vitest-cli-agent-end.log`: 2 files passed, 4 tests passed, 70 skipped by focused filter.
  - `diff-check.log`: no whitespace errors; diff is limited to `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` and `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts`.
  - `openclaw-start.log` / `openclaw-dev-gateway.log`: dev gateway reported `ready` on `http://127.0.0.1:19175` with 8 plugins loaded.
  - `real-gateway-delayed-autocapture-proof.md`: a real Gateway agent final response returned while `github-pr-workflow` proposals were still 0; detached Skill Research auto-capture then wrote a real pending `github-pr-workflow` proposal 17.815s after final.
- **Observed result after fix:** The new harness regression proves `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` resolves without resolving a pending Skill Research auto-capture promise, while still invoking the awaited plugin hook path. The CLI regression still proves local CLI runs wait for plugin `agent_end` hooks and channel-backed CLI runs do not. The real Gateway run exercises delayed Skill Research auto-capture after the patch: final response returned first, then capture completed later.
- **What was not tested:** External channel, Desktop, Mantis, private CI, and third-party account flows were not exercised. The real behavior proof uses local Gateway with a mock model endpoint so the delayed capture path runs without external account credentials.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.

### Redacted delayed auto-capture log excerpt

```text
[2026-06-03T07:27:43.807Z] configured mock OpenAI model, gateway auth none, and skills.workshop.autonomous.enabled=true
[2026-06-03T07:27:51.483Z] prepopulated 8000 workshop proposals outside active workspace
[2026-06-03T07:28:00.269Z] github-pr-workflow proposals before agent run: 0
[2026-06-03T07:28:18.722Z] END gateway-agent: exit=0
[2026-06-03T07:28:18.745Z] gateway agent final returned in 18447ms; github-pr-workflow proposals immediately after final: 0
[2026-06-03T07:28:36.561Z] github-pr-workflow proposal observed after final wait: count=1 elapsedMs=17815
2026-06-03T15:28:18.587+08:00 OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_87504_GATEWAY
2026-06-03T15:28:36.385+08:00 [skills/research] skill research auto-capture queued workshop proposal github-pr-workflow
```

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: Fixed with redacted terminal/log proof from a real local Gateway run. The run used `openclaw gateway run` plus `openclaw gateway call agent --expect-final`, with Skill Workshop autonomous capture enabled and a busy real workshop store to delay capture.
- RF-002: Fixed by adding real Gateway proof to this PR body. The final agent response returned first, `github-pr-workflow` proposal count was still 0 immediately after final, and the detached capture wrote the proposal 17.815s later.
- RF-003: Fixed by replacing the earlier unit/mock-only proof gap with a real Gateway session that exercised delayed Skill Research auto-capture after the patch.
- RF-004: Fixed with live availability proof outside the mocked harness: the Gateway final response returned before delayed capture completed, showing the awaited response path no longer waits for that capture work.
- RF-005: Addressed as a proof/body refresh only. The latest blocker did not require additional code repair; the remaining maintainer merge decision stays outside this contributor update.

## Merge risk

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users closing routine local CLI sessions should not wait on Skill Research capture work before the session can finish. The original PR carried an availability risk because it extended a hook timeout; this rebased patch instead removes capture work from the awaited session-close path.
- **What did NOT change:** Only `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts` and its focused harness regression changed. This PR does not change public config, schema, protocol, storage/data format, model transcript shape, CLI flags, plugin SDK contracts, or default Skill Workshop enablement; external channel/Desktop/Mantis/private CI paths are out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Current `origin/main` removed the old `extensions/skill-workshop` runtime. The active source-of-truth for this behavior is the current agent harness side-effect path calling `runSkillResearchAutoCapture` from `src/skills/research/autocapture.ts`; the plugin `agent_end` hook wait boundary remains owned by `awaitAgentHarnessAgentEndHook`.
- **Architecture boundary:** Skill Research capture is side-effect work and now always starts detached with caught/logged failures. Plugin `agent_end` hooks keep the previous caller-sensitive behavior: awaited for local CLI callers that require hook completion, fire-and-forget for channel-backed paths.
- **Related open PR scan:** This is an existing PR repair for #87504, not a new same-contract competing PR. The review checklist items RF-001 through RF-005 are mapped above, and the rebased diff targets the current harness path rather than restoring deleted extension code.
- **Out of scope:** External channel, Desktop, Mantis, private CI, third-party account flows, timeout budget changes, old extension restoration, and public contract changes are not part of this patch.
- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: session-state`, and `merge-risk: compatibility` were considered because this touches agent-end lifecycle scheduling. No public config, schema, protocol, storage format, or model transcript shape changes are introduced.
- **Risk explanation:** The changed boundary is `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.ts`: Skill Research auto-capture starts asynchronously, while the plugin `agent_end` hook path keeps the previous await/fire-and-forget distinction. The session-state risk is limited to when capture work completes, not what messages are sent to capture or how terminal agent state is recorded.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is two files and preserves caught/logged capture failures. It does not raise timeout budgets, does not add fallback behavior, and the focused CLI regression confirms awaited plugin hooks are still awaited for local CLI runs. The real Gateway proof also shows delayed capture completing after the final agent response.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 91/100. Fresh tests cover the exact scheduling invariant, local gateway startup proves the current worktree still boots, and a real Gateway agent run demonstrates delayed Skill Research capture after final response. The remaining untested items are external channel/Desktop/Mantis paths outside this harness-only change.
- **Patch quality notes:** The `.catch` is the same error-containment behavior as the previous `try/catch`, moved onto the detached promise so capture failures remain logged without blocking session close. This is a root-cause scheduling fix, not a mitigation or fallback; no lockfiles or unrelated files changed.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` is the primary regression target; `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` provides the nearest caller-level proof for CLI `agent_end` await behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added a regression in `src/agents/harness/agent-end-side-effects.test.ts` that keeps Skill Research auto-capture pending and proves `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` still resolves.
- Re-ran the focused CLI `agent_end` reliability cases to prove local CLI runs still await plugin hooks, while channel-backed runs stay fire-and-forget.
- Re-ran `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` and a local dev gateway startup from the current PR worktree.
- Ran a real local Gateway agent turn with delayed Skill Research auto-capture enabled; final response returned before the pending `github-pr-workflow` proposal appeared, and the proposal was observed 17.815s later.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `awaitAgentEndSideEffects` called `await runCoreAgentEndSideEffects(...)`, and that helper awaited `runSkillResearchAutoCapture(...)` before plugin hooks could settle. Local CLI runs intentionally use the awaited agent-end path, so any slow Skill Research capture work stayed on the routine session-close path.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scheduling invariant now matches the desired lifecycle boundary: Skill Research capture always starts as a side effect with caught/logged failures, and only the plugin `agent_end` hook path is awaited when callers explicitly require hook completion. This removes the capture wait from the session-close path instead of increasing timeout budgets or adding a fallback.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
